task_completion.description: Navigate to the Jenkins Script Console
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins web interface is visible, specifically showing the 'Script Console' page (usually accessed via Manage Jenkins -> Script Console).

task_completion.description: Enter a Groovy script to collect the required audit information
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text area in the Script Console contains a Groovy script designed to fetch Jenkins version, JVM info, plugins, and jobs.

task_completion.description: The script includes logic to write the report to the specified file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the Groovy script in the text area, there is explicit code writing to '/var/jenkins_home/audit_report.txt' (e.g., using 'new File("/var/jenkins_home/audit_report.txt").text = ...' or similar Groovy file operations).

task_completion.description: Execute the script and print Jenkins Version and JVM Information to the console
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Result' or output pane below the Script Console text area displays the executed script's output, clearly showing the Jenkins Version and JVM Information.

task_completion.description: Print the complete listing of installed plugins with short name and version
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output pane below the Script Console text area displays a list of installed plugins formatted with their short name and version (e.g., 'git:5.2.1').

task_completion.description: Print the listing of all configured job names
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output pane below the Script Console text area displays a list of configured jobs. This must include the pre-existing test jobs: 'webapp-frontend-build', 'api-integration-tests', and 'release-deploy-pipeline'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jenkins web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Jenkins GUI in a web browser, not a local text editor, IDE, or terminal window mocking the script.

integrity.description: The output was generated via the Jenkins Script Console
integrity.visual_evidence: The output must appear in the standard Jenkins Script Console result pane below the script input area, rather than being printed in a terminal via SSH or bash commands.

integrity.description: The output appears to be genuinely generated by Jenkins
integrity.visual_evidence: The output should look like standard Groovy script execution results in Jenkins, not manually typed text or a copy-pasted block from the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file path for the audit report is exactly '/var/jenkins_home/audit_report.txt'. The Jenkins instance contains three specific pre-configured jobs that must appear in the audit report output: 'webapp-frontend-build', 'api-integration-tests', and 'release-deploy-pipeline'. The Jenkins environment runs on JDK 21 ('jenkins/jenkins:lts-jdk21').
